title: -Contract with Wildfire Defense to Serve as the Alameda County Coordinator

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Authorizing the City Manager or designee to execute a contract with Wildfire Defense
to serve as the Alameda County Coordinator for coordination with local agencies, fire
safe councils, and wildfire mitigation community groups for the initial period August 1,
2025, through June 30, 2026, in an amount not to exceed $155,500, and to apply for
and receive future grant funding in collaboration with Berkeley Fire Department staff to
maintain the position through 2028; and
2. Authorizing the City Manager or designee to execute any amendments for not more
than $225,000 per year in Fiscal Year (FY) 25, FY26, and FY27, upon receiving
additional California Fire Safe Council (CFSC) grants to continue contracting Wildfire
Defense to serve Alameda County communities, provide resources and education on
wildfire safety, promote collaboration, assist local groups with funding opportunities, and
aid multi-jurisdictional planning.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for this contract is provided by the California Fire Safe Council (CFSC) County
Coordinator Grant Program for FY 2025–2026 [Fund Number: 336-72-741-831-0000-
000-422-612990-]. As there are no matching funds required, there is no impact to the
City’s General Fund. Staff will seek additional grant funding to sustain the position
beyond July 2026.
sections.current_situation:
Alameda County faces persistent and significant wildfire risk, particularly in the wildland-
urban interface areas such as the Berkeley, Oakland Hills, and Hayward. Recent years
have seen increased wildfire activity across California, highlighting the urgent need for
coordinated, countywide mitigation and preparedness efforts. While the region is home
to a number of active wildfire mitigation groups and agencies, these efforts have
historically been fragmented, with limited formal collaboration or shared strategic
planning.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

Contract with Wildfire Defense to Serve as the Alameda County Coordinator CONSENT CALENDAR
July 29, 2025
The California Fire Safe Council funds county coordinator positions, and Alameda
County is one of six counties that did not have a Coordinator position prior to FY25. The
position is housed within Fire Department and actively coordinates and collaborates
with county-wide community groups, fire councils, HOAs, and other mitigation support
groups. The City of Berkeley issued a Request for Proposals (RFP) to contract with an
individual that will serve as the Alameda County Coordinator. The chosen vendor will
work in partnership with all Alameda County jurisdictions to complete the required
deliverables as set forth by the grantor and develop a work plan to further coordinate
wildfire mitigation activities with a budget not to exceed $155,500 for the current period
of performance ending in July 2026. The City Council approved the formal bids and
solicitation request for Specification No. 25-11738-C at the June 3, 2025, City Council
meeting. The RFP was then posted on the City's Bids and Solicitation site on June 17,
2025. Responses to questions were made available on the City’s website on June 23,
2025. The deadline for proposal submissions was July 8, 2025, re
sections.background:
The County Coordinator program, funded by the California Fire Safe Council (CFSC), is
designed to address the need for countywide collaboration and strategic alignment. The
Coordinator will serve as a central point of contact, connecting wildfire mitigation
groups, supporting grant applications, and facilitating the use of advanced planning
tools such as the pathways analysis. The Coordinator will also be responsible for
fulfilling specific grant deliverables and building regional capacity while developing a
sustainable framework for ongoing collaboration.
During the previous County Coordinator grant period, the City of Berkeley was unable to
hire a full-time County Coordinator due to delays in funding availability, resulting in only
interim, volunteer-based coordination. This gap limited the county’s ability to build
sustained relationships, leverage data-driven planning tools, and increase the flow of
mitigation funding to high-risk communities. There remains a clear need for a
Page 2
Page 3 of 4
Internal
Contract with Wildfire Defense to Serve as the Alameda County Coordinator CONSENT CALENDAR
July 29, 2025
professional, dedicated County Coordinator to unify wildfire mitigation groups, facilitate
project implementation, and ensure compliance with state and federal grant
requirements.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Ensuring the Department is maximizing available resources and working with other
county coordinators to collaborate on regional mitigation activities will help sustain the
local environment and mitigate the potential of fire, mudslides, and other catastrophic
impacts associated with the overgrowth of vegetation and loss of habitat post-wildfire.
sections.rationale:
Wildfire Defense is uniquely qualified to coordinate countywide wildfire mitigation efforts,
having demonstrated expertise in wildfire planning, grant administration, and
stakeholder engagement. This contract ensures continuity of critical wildfire resilience
work and positions the City and County to secure future funding.
sections.alternatives:
None.
CONTACT PERSON
Shanalee Gallagher, Principal Program Manager, Fire Department (510) 981-3473
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
Internal
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: WILDFIRE DEFENSE TO SERVE AS THE ALAMEDA COUNTY
COORDINATOR
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley and Alameda County have prioritized wildfire mitigation
and resilience; and
WHEREAS, Alameda County Board of Supervisors provided City of Berkeley support to
apply for and manage a county-wide position; and
WHEREAS, the California Fire Safe Council (CFSC) County Coordinator Grant Program
provides funding to support countywide wildfire mitigation coordination; and
WHEREAS, Wildfire Defense has been selected and is qualified to serve as County
Coordinator, facilitating partnerships, ensuring deliverables, and securing future grant
funding.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager or designee is authorized to execute a contract with Wildfire Defense to
serve as the Alameda County Coordinator for coordination with local agencies, fire safe
councils, and wildfire mitigation community groups for the initial period August 1, 2025,
through June 30, 2026, in an amount not to exceed $155,500, [Fund Number: 336-72-
741-831-0000-000-422-612990-]. and to apply for and receive future grant funding in
collaboration with Berkeley Fire Department staff to maintain the position through 2028.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the City Manager or designee is authorized to execute
any amendments for not more than $225,000 per year in Fiscal Year (FY) 25, FY26, and
FY27, upon receiving additional California Fire Safe Council (CFSC) grants to contract
Wildfire Defense to serve Alameda County communities, provide resources and
education on wildfire safety, promote collaboration, assist local groups with funding
opportunities, and aid multi-jurisdictional planning.
